deck: [Zap, Dualcast, Coolheaded, Compile Driver, Sweeping Beam, Leap, Strike x4, Defend x4]
cause_of_death: Jaw Worm at Floor 7 with 5 HP remaining after barely surviving Gremlin Nob elite at Floor 6.
key_moments: First ever Defect run. Learned core orb mechanics: Lightning (3 passive/8 evoke), Frost (2 passive/5 evoke), channeling, evoking, Dualcast, Focus stat, orb slots.
Lost 18 HP in first fight (Louse pair at Floor 1) — unfamiliarity with Defect's combat rhythm and orb timing.
World of Goop event at Floor 2: took 75 gold for 11 HP at 57/75 HP (correct trade at high HP).

Entered Gremlin Nob elite at 46/75 HP with a Skill-heavy deck — 7 Skills (4 Defend + Coolheaded + Leap + Zap) vs 5 Attacks (4 Strike + Compile Driver). Sweeping Beam is an Attack but was added post-Nob.
Nob's Enrage punished every Skill played. Defect's starting deck is naturally Skill-heavy (Zap is a Skill, Dualcast is a Skill), making this the worst possible elite matchup.
Barely survived Nob at 5 HP — entered Floor 7 Jaw Worm with no margin for error and died.
lessons: Defect's starting deck has 7 Skills vs 3 Attacks (4 Defend + Zap + Dualcast + Coolheaded vs 4 Strike). This is the most Skill-heavy starter of any character. Gremlin Nob is the worst possible elite matchup.

Defect MUST prioritize Attack card pickups in early floors to rebalance the deck before any elite path. Compile Driver (Attack, 1E, 7 damage + draw 1 per orb) and Sweeping Beam (Attack, 1E, 6 AOE + draw 1) are excellent early Attack pickups.
Focus cards (Defragment, Consume) should be top priority for Defect scaling — Focus increases passive and evoke values of ALL orbs permanently.

Orb mechanics learned: Lightning orb passively deals 3 damage to random enemy at end of turn, evokes for 8. Frost orb passively gives 2 block, evokes for 5. Cracked Core starter relic channels 1 Lightning at combat start.
Horn Cleat (14 block on Turn 2) was the only thing that kept the Nob fight survivable.
